claims.match: There is no empty `contract.sql` for ceremony's sake.
claims.summary: A nullable additive column is emitted in expand without an empty contract phase.

claims.match: developer supplies the historical value; onwardpg keeps that edit ahead of its
claims.summary: A required column is added nullable, reconciled explicitly, gated, and tightened after drain.
Expand may temporarily loosen enforcement so legacy and new writes coexist.

claims.match: onwardpg creates a temporary second column
claims.summary: A confirmed same-type rename can use a deterministic bidirectional bridge and final native rename.
An unproved dependent-view rename stays inside three ordered, dependency-scoped edit pockets.

claims.match: closures. Exactly two edit pockets remain
claims.summary: A confirmed cross-name/type transition owns both endpoint columns and the entire dependent closure.

claims.match: dependency order for recreation, materialized-view population state, index
claims.summary: Materialized dependencies and their indexes are ordered and freshness remains an explicit product boundary.
Plain plan reruns keep one feature identity while accepted history changes underneath it.

claims.match: remain visible. Omit `dev_database_env` entirely when this optional D → W view
claims.summary: Development inspection is optional and its observer-only ACL overlay is not durable drift.

claims.match: Contract is now an evidence boundary rather than a phase-name convention.
claims.summary: Contract cleanup is blocked on exact live catalog, data, and expiring writer evidence.

claims.match: JSON is the default and each document leads with a status or error code.
claims.summary: Agent output exposes status, copyable decisions, edit paths, and scoped remediation.

claims.match: the final semantic diff is empty
claims.summary: Disposable verification requires exact execution and an empty final semantic residual.

claims.match: deployments, onwardpg says so instead of hiding one inside a phase name.
claims.summary: A transition that cannot fit one rolling deployment is split or refused rather than mislabeled safe.

scenarios.title: Add a nullable column without ceremonial contract SQL
scenarios.protected_invariant: The generated bundle adds the nullable column in expand, writes no empty contract phase, and converges.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: Legacy SQL cannot reference the new nullable column and no existing write contract is tightened.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: The generated scenario has no contract work or live readiness gate.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: The generated scenario has no contract work or live readiness gate.

scenarios.title: Add a future-required column while old writers omit it
scenarios.protected_invariant: Expand stays nullable; reviewed cleanup precedes the exact NULL gate and final NOT NULL enforcement.

scenarios.title: Widen a finite CHECK in expand only
scenarios.protected_invariant: Old and new values are accepted after expand and no later tightening is invented.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: A proven widening is complete in expand and has no contract gate.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: A proven widening is complete in expand and has no contract gate.

scenarios.title: Temporarily remove incompatible CHECK enforcement and restore it after cleanup
scenarios.protected_invariant: Neither old nor new writers are rejected in overlap; contamination blocks restoration until reviewed cleanup succeeds.

scenarios.title: Keep prepared legacy SQL and new SQL alive through a two-way column bridge
scenarios.protected_invariant: One-sided old and new writes synchronize, conflicting dual writes fail, and physical cutover waits for drain and equality.

scenarios.title: Rename a column while preserving the old view output prefix
scenarios.protected_invariant: The overlap view is legal PostgreSQL, old prepared view reads survive, and drop/cutover/recreation stays dependency ordered.

scenarios.title: Change a column name and type beneath views, a materialized view, and an index
scenarios.protected_invariant: Explicit identity owns both columns and the full closure; reviewed conversion handles malformed and overflow values without stray generated DDL.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: The current native owner proves the compatibility handoff and final convergence, but a compiled live-readiness gate for this family remains planned.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: The current native owner proves the compatibility handoff and final convergence, but a compiled live-readiness gate for this family remains planned.

scenarios.title: Rebuild a dependent materialized view and index with explicit freshness
scenarios.protected_invariant: Dependency destruction is reversed, recreation is forward ordered, and freshness is never inferred from catalog shape alone.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: The current native owner proves dependency ordering, the explicit freshness handoff, and convergence; compiled old-client execution remains planned.

expanded_new.omitted_reason: The current native owner proves dependency ordering, the explicit freshness handoff, and convergence; compiled new-client execution remains planned.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: A compiled live-readiness refusal for materialized-view refresh remains planned.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: A compiled live-readiness authorization for materialized-view refresh remains planned.

scenarios.title: Restack one active feature on a changed accepted-history head
scenarios.protected_invariant: PlanID remains stable, generation advances only for material change, and accepted upstream work is not absorbed from the development database.

pglite.reason: Restacking requires compiled CLI state, accepted-history replay, and independent native PostgreSQL databases.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: The restack owner proves generated SQL and workspace scoping; release client execution is owned by the release-contract scenarios.

expanded_new.omitted_reason: The restack owner proves generated SQL and workspace scoping; release client execution is owned by the release-contract scenarios.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: This canonical restack variant uses an additive feature result; gated restack variants are separate planned matrix rows.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: This canonical restack variant uses an additive feature result; gated restack variants are separate planned matrix rows.

scenarios.title: Plan without a development database and normalize a dedicated read-only observer
scenarios.protected_invariant: Scratch-only planning works; observer access is projected away while genuine application grants remain visible.

pglite.reason: Roles, ownership, ACLs, and separate observer backends are outside the PGlite authority boundary.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: Development reconciliation is read-only catalog inspection and does not apply a release expand phase.

expanded_new.omitted_reason: Development reconciliation is read-only catalog inspection and does not apply a release expand phase.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: Development reconciliation cannot authorize or execute production contract.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: Development reconciliation cannot authorize or execute production contract.

scenarios.title: Refuse contract until live writer and data evidence matches the exact plan
scenarios.protected_invariant: One repeatable-read, read-only snapshot checks the receipted expand catalog, data gates, and expiring writer cohorts before cleanup.

pglite.reason: Writer drain evidence and independent read-only backend behavior require native PostgreSQL.

scenarios.title: Keep optional development reconciliation actionable when product SQL is required
scenarios.protected_invariant: A blocking development SQL handoff exposes cumulative inspection argv and the same resolution in JSON and text without changing the durable bundle.

pglite.reason: The receipt covers compiled high-level and low-level CLI handoff behavior against an independent development database.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: This protocol receipt stops at the optional development SQL handoff; release-contract scenarios own expand clients.

expanded_new.omitted_reason: This protocol receipt stops at the optional development SQL handoff; release-contract scenarios own expand clients.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: The durable bundle and its contract lifecycle are independently covered by the required-column scenario.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: The durable bundle and its contract lifecycle are independently covered by the required-column scenario.

converged.omitted_reason: The receipt proves actionable development remediation, not execution of caller-owned local SQL.

scenarios.title: Expose findings, written edit paths, and copyable next actions
scenarios.protected_invariant: Default JSON and text preserve safety findings; edit actions name existing receipts; commands are shell-copyable and scoped.

pglite.reason: This is a compiled CLI protocol contract, not a phase-SQL engine variant.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: The protocol-only scenario stops before phase execution; release scenarios own legacy SQL checks.

expanded_new.omitted_reason: The protocol-only scenario stops before phase execution; release scenarios own new SQL checks.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: The protocol-only scenario validates remediation rendering rather than live readiness.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: The protocol-only scenario validates remediation rendering rather than live readiness.

converged.omitted_reason: The protocol-only scenario validates remediation rendering; native release owners prove convergence.

scenarios.title: Require another deployment when one rolling compatibility window is unproved
scenarios.protected_invariant: The planner does not disguise an unsupported enum identity transition as safe expand/contract SQL.

pglite.reason: The protected invariant is planner refusal and deployment-count honesty, not executable phase compatibility.

expanded_legacy.omitted_reason: Refusal prevents generation of a misleading one-rollout expand phase.

expanded_new.omitted_reason: Refusal prevents generation of a misleading one-rollout expand phase.

contract_blocked.omitted_reason: No contract is generated until the product is split into compatible deployments.

contract_ready.omitted_reason: No contract is generated until the product is split into compatible deployments.

converged.omitted_reason: The refusal is the expected result; convergence belongs to the later intermediate and cleanup plans.

scenarios.title: Combine rename, future-required column, incompatible CHECK, drain, cleanup, and final proof
scenarios.protected_invariant: The composed plan preserves prepared legacy SQL and new SQL after expand, rejects bridge conflicts, blocks early cleanup, and removes every compatibility artifact.
